intro: The clearest split between Amazon SES and Lob is how they charge: Amazon SES uses a free tier plus paid plans starting around $50 per month, while Lob uses subscription pricing starting around $260/mo. Amazon SES is AWS scalable transactional and bulk email sending service with SMTP and API access; it fits best for Transactional emails (receipts, alerts). Lob is Messaging API (Email / SMS / Voice) tool for developers. Specializes in Direct Mail API; it fits best for Transactional emails (receipts, alerts). Pick Amazon SES for Transactional emails (receipts, alerts); pick Lob for Transactional emails (receipts, alerts). Amazon SES is not for internal projects. Lob is not for internal projects. Honest trade-offs: Amazon SES is weak for internal team communication, and Lob is weak for internal team communication — match to your real scenario. Both sit under the Messaging API (Email / SMS / Voice) umbrella, so your final call depends on pricing transparency, integration effort, and which listed not-for situations you can actually tolerate.
quick_take: Amazon SES is for transactional emails (receipts, alerts); Lob is for transactional emails (receipts, alerts); decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Amazon SES if you want a free tier plus paid plans starting around $50 per month and your workload matches transactional emails (receipts, alerts).
verdict_b: Choose Lob if you want subscription pricing starting around $260/mo and your workload matches transactional emails (receipts, alerts).

faq.q: Is Amazon SES cheaper than Lob?
faq.a: A direct comparison depends on your usage profile and which pricing model fits better. Amazon SES has a free tier with paid plans starting at $50 per month. Lob uses subscription pricing starting at $260/mo.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Amazon SES to Lob?
faq.a: Migration may be non-trivial: Amazon SES is rated high lock-in (SMTP-compatible, portable to other email providers). Plan for data export, re-integration of SDKs, and re-testing flows before cutover. Verify exportable formats and feature parity against your live use cases before committing.

Both score 4/5 on developer experience in our data. Evaluate against your own stack: read their docs, run a quick SDK spike, and check error handling. Self-assessing with a small prototype beats trusting any rating at face value.

faq.q: Is Lob a good alternative to Amazon SES?
faq.a: Lob sits in the same category as Amazon SES, so it can be a reasonable alternative for overlapping use cases. Lob is best fit for transactional emails (receipts, alerts). Note: Lob is not for internal projects.
